teacher_guide.big_idea: Students need a clear organizer while rotating through stations so they can compare what each station reveals before the closing task.
learning_goals: I can record evidence from more than one station.
I can compare patterns across stations.
I can use an organizer to keep evidence readable.

timing.activity: Explain the station flow and organizer purpose.
Students rotate through stations and record evidence.
Students compare evidence in the organizer and task sheet.

teacher_notes: Keep the organizer focused on comparing evidence rather than copying every detail from a station card.
Push students to notice patterns across stations before the exit response.

content.subtitle: Use the organizer to compare what each station shows before you write your final idea.

rows: Record one important piece of evidence from each station.
Use the organizer to compare what repeats or changes.
Use your organizer before writing the exit response.

task_sheet.title: Station Task Sheet
instructions: Move through the stations in order.
Record one strong detail from each station before you move on.
Use the organizer to compare what you noticed.

tasks.label: Station notes
tasks.prompt: Record the strongest evidence from each station in short point form.

tasks.label: Compare
tasks.prompt: What pattern or contrast do you notice when you look across the stations?

embedded_supports: Underline repeated evidence across more than one station.
success_criteria: I recorded evidence from the stations.
I compared what I noticed across stations.

graphic_organizer.title: Station Comparison Organizer
graphic_organizer.prompt: Use this organizer to compare evidence from each station before writing your exit response.
success_criteria: I used the organizer to compare evidence, not just collect it.

exit_ticket.title: Exit Ticket
exit_ticket.prompt: What is one pattern you noticed across the stations, and what evidence supports it?
success_criteria: I named one pattern and one supporting piece of evidence.
